G-Protein Coupled Receptor Expression along the Mouse Renal Tubule (RNA-seq)

G-protein coupled receptors (GPCRs) are a diverse group of seven-transmembrane domain receptors that bind to molecular ligands that activate the associated G-protein, leading to downstream activation of cellular processes.

Please cite: Poll BG, Chen L, Chou CL, Raghuram V, Knepper MA. Landscape of GPCR expression along the mouse nephron. Am J Physiol Renal Physiol. 2021; 321: F50-F68. PMID: 34029142

The database includes 758 genes. Click to download processed data as spreadsheet.

Methods: Adult mouse kidneys were dissected and single cell RNA seq was performed as described in Chen et al. 2021 (PMID: 33769951). A curated list of mouse GPCRs was created by combining data from Ensembl, MGI, and IUPHAR. The complete GPCR list is shown here. This list was mapped to the RNA-seq results, presented in the table below.

Nephron Terminology: PTS1, the initial segment of the proximal tubule; PTS2, proximal straight tubule in cortical medullary rays; PTS3, last segment of the proximal straight tubule in the outer stripe of outer medulla; DTL1, the short descending limb of the loop of Henle; DTL2, long descending limb of the loop of Henle in the outer medulla; DTL3, long descending limb of the loop of Henle in the inner medulla; ATL, thin ascending limb of the loop of Henle; MTAL, medullary thick ascending limb of the loop of Henle; CTAL, cortical thick ascending limb of the loop of Henle; DCT, distal convoluted tubule; CNT, connecting tubule; CCD, cortical collecting duct; OMCD, outer medullary collecting duct; IMCD, inner medullary collecting duct; Glom, Glomerulus.

To search, use browser's Find command (Ctrl-F).

Gene symbol (mouse) Annotation PTS1 PTS2 PTS3 DTL1 DTL2 DTL3 ATL MTAL CTAL DCT CNT CCD OMCD IMCD Glom (mean)
